sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal, prop. to be before, in front, over against (Arab. قَبْلُ front, قَبْلَ before); hence, to come from before, to come to meet, Arab. قَبَلَ.

Piel קִבֵּל.-

(1) prop. to receive any one (one who comes to meet one, Arab. قَبلَ), 1Ch 12:18 any thing, i.q. לָקַח, but only in the later [?] Hebr., 2Ch 29:16, 22 2Ch 29:22 Ezr 8:30 (opp. to reject); Job 2:10 Est 4:4 9:23 , 27 to receive instruction, Pro 19:20.

Hiphil, intransitive, to be opposed, stand over against one another, Exo 26:5 36:12 . Arab. Conj. III. id.

Derivatives, קֳבָל, קֹבֶל.
